title: Wheat Yellow Rust Management (ICAR-IIWBR)
content: Yellow rust (Puccinia striiformis) is a major fungal disease in the North Western Reform Plain Zone. Symptoms include yellow pustules in linear rows (stripes) on leaves. Management: Apply Propiconazole 25 EC @ 0.1% (1ml in 1 liter of water) or Tebuconazole 250 EC @ 0.1%. Ensure specialized boom sprayers maintain a speed of 4-6 km/h for uniform coverage.

title: Mustard Aphid Control (DRMR)
content: Aphids (Lipaphis erysimi) cause significant yield loss. Economic Threshold Level (ETL): 20-25 aphids per 10 cm terminal shoot. Management: Spray Oxydemeton methyl 25 EC @ 0.025% or Dimethoate 30 EC @ 0.03%. Prefer spraying in late afternoons to protect honeybee pollinators.

title: Rice Blast Management (ICAR-NRRI)
content: Rice blast (Magnaporthe oryzae) affects leaves, nodes, and panicles. Symptoms: Diamond-shaped lesions with gray centers. Early management: Avoid excessive Nitrogen. Spray Tricyclazole 75 WP @ 0.6 g/L or Carbendazim 50 WP @ 1.0 g/L.

title: Precision Spraying & Safety (CIBRC)
content: Pesticide Application Safety: 1. Use Personal Protective Equipment (PPE). 2. Calibrate boom sprayers weekly. 3. Check nozzle health. 4. Wind speed must be below 10 km/h to prevent drift. 5. Maintain boom height exactly 50 cm above crop canopy for 110-degree flat fan nozzles.
